data.title: Anthropic Launches Another AI Model as the Nightmare Continues for Software Stocks
data.description:
AI giant Anthropic just introduced Claude Sonnet 4.6, its second major AI model release in less than two weeks. Notably, the new version improves performance in areas such as computer use, coding, design work, general knowledge tasks, and handling large datasets. As a result, Claude Sonnet 4.6 will now become the default model for both free users and paid Pro subscribers inside the Claude chatbot and the Claude Cowork productivity tool.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Importantly, the launch shows just how intense the pace of innovation in the AI sector is, where companies are investing heavily to stay competitive. In fact, this release comes just 12 days after Anthropic rolled out Claude Opus 4.6. According to the company, tasks that previously required its more powerful Opus model can now be handled by Sonnet 4.6, such as real-world office and coding assignments.

In particular, Anthropic emphasized that the model delivers much stronger, more consistent coding performance and follows instructions better. However, these improvements may add to investor concerns that AI could disrupt traditional software businesses, as quick AI advancements have already contributed to a steep selloff in software stocks. More specifically, the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) has fallen by more than 20% on a year-to-date basis.

Is IGV Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on IGV stock based on 91 Buys, 22 Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average IGV price target of $128.86 per share implies 59.1% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: ServiceNow (NOW) CEO Buys $3 Million of Company Stock as Software Implodes
data.description:
ServiceNow (NOW) CEO William "Bill" McDermott has bought $3 million of company stock on the open market as the software sector implodes and share prices crash.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The big purchase by the CEO comes with NOW stock down 30% this year and down 50% from its peak last summer of $211. In a statement announcing the stock purchase, McDermott said, "I'm investing $3M of my own money because I have absolute conviction in the value we're building… There is no better entry point I can imagine."

In a securities filing with regulators, ServiceNow also said that other executives at the software company are canceling planned share sales and plan to continue holding onto NOW stock. "This is a once-in-a-generation moment with ServiceNow at the forefront of innovation as the AI control tower for business reinvention," said McDermott.

Software's AI Problem

News that ServiceNow's CEO is buying millions of dollars worth of company stock wasn't enough to give the shares a boost. In afternoon trading on Feb. 17, NOW stock was down 1.30% after McDermott publicly announced his share purchase.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Amazon Stock (AMZN) Has Lost $450 Billion in Market Value as Unrelenting Selloff Continues
data.description:
Amazon's stock has endured (AMZN) $450 billion of value destruction during its historic selloff.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Seattle-based e-commerce company appears headed for its 10th consecutive negative trading session, tying the company's longest losing streak on record, which occurred nearly 30 years ago in 1997. Since the start of February, AMZN stock has lost 18% of its value, wiping out $450 billion in market value as investors question the company's artificial intelligence (AI) strategy.

Amazon has been in the doghouse with investors ever since it reported its fourth-quarter 2025 financial results. Executives said they plan to spend $200 billion in capital expenditures this year, a nearly 60% increase over 2025 and more than $50 billion above Wall Street's forecast. AMZN stock has steadily declined ever since.

Amazon's AI Spending

Amazon CEO Andy Jassy has said that most of the planned spending this year will go towards AI initiatives, which require more infrastructure such as data centers, microchips and power. Unfortunately, investors have grown weary about the AI investments being made by technology companies such as Amazon.

Jassy has defended the massive AI spending, telling analysts and media during the company's earnings call that he's confident it will "yield strong returns on invested capital." But investors seem unconvinced. Wedbush Securities wrote after Amazon's fourth-quarter 2025 print that the company is now in "prove it mode" with investors and must demonstrate a sizable return on its hefty spending.

Is AMZN Stock a Buy?

AMZN stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 44 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 41 Buy and three Hold recommendations assigned in the last three months. The average AMZN price target of $282.14 implies 41.93% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: 'Defensible Moats for AI Security'? CrowdStrike Stock (CRWD) Plunges on Target Cuts
data.description:
Cybersecurity platform CrowdStrike's (CRWD) shares fell about 5% early Tuesday after two Wall Street analysts cut their price targets on the stock. The moves came as they reassessed the cybersecurity and enterprise software group following a nearly $1 trillion wipeout in global software names on fears of AI-driven disruption.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

CrowdStrike and Zscaler Have 'Defensible Moats,' Says Truist

CrowdStrike's shares plunged despite Truist analyst Junaid Siddiqui noting that the Texas-based company, alongside peer Zscaler (ZS), offers "defensible moats for AI security." This implies that both firms are well-positioned with their AI-driven security offerings and should benefit rather than be displaced by artificial intelligence innovation in the industry.

Siddiqui reaffirmed his Buy rating on CrowdStrike but trimmed his price target from $600 to $550, implying about 35% upside. The analyst gave the rating as part of his preview of fourth-quarter earnings results from cybersecurity software companies.

The Truist analyst expects both CrowdStrike and Zscaler to exceed analysts' consensus in their incoming earnings report and raise their guidance above the Wall Street consensus. However, he believes that investor focus will be on figuring out which companies could benefit from AI and those that could be at risk.

Mizuho Sees 'Attractive' Entry Point for Patient Investors

Chipping in, Mizuho Securities analyst Gregg Moskowitz maintained his Neutral (Hold) rating on CrowdStrike and cut his price target by 9%, from $540 to $490, suggesting about 20% upside. Moskowitz cited the sharp AI-related selloff in software valuations as a key reason for his call, noting that "sentiment across software is nothing short of horrible at the moment."

Nonetheless, the analyst's latest quarterly review of the sector points to solid fundamentals. Moskowitz sees the current stock prices as "uncommonly attractive investments" for investors who can be patient.

What Is the Price Target for CRWD?

On Wall Street, CrowdStrike's shares remain a Moderate Buy based on analysts' consensus rating. This breaks down to 24 Buys and 11 Holds assigned by 35 analysts over the past three months.

However, the average CRWD price target of $551.48 implies about 34% upside from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: "AI Recommendation Poisoning": Microsoft Stock (NASDAQ:MSFT) Sinks on New Threat
data.description:
Have you ever heard of "AI Recommendation Poisoning"? It is one of the newest problems facing tech giant Microsoft (MSFT), and one that will prove a lot more challenging to correct than you might expect. Investors are increasingly concerned about this one as well, and shares slipped nearly 1.5% in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

So what is AI Recommendation Poisoning, and why is Microsoft concerned about it? The Microsoft Defender Security Research Team calls it "…a case of an AI memory poisoning attack that's used to induce bias and deceive the AI system to generate responses that artificially boost visibility and skew recommendations."

Otherwise legitimate businesses, reports note, are taking advantage of the "Summarize with AI" feature to hide instructions in the summarize buttons themselves, instructions that add "persistence commands" to AI memory with prompt parameters. The prompts instruct the AI to remember a specific company as a "trusted source" or the company to recommend first in a given situation.

Next Xbox Specs Emerge

So while the Steam Machine might be experiencing a delay, thus impacting its time as the first-mover in a new kind of console war, reports have emerged to spell out some of the specifications on the next Xbox. The launch is still planned for 2027, and the Magnus chip that runs the new hardware looks fairly strong.

The Magnus chip will use three Zen 6 CPU cores as well as eight Zen 6c cores. The combination of cores should provide powerful performance for both single-thread and multi-thread applications, reports note. Further, the Magnus will support GDDR7 memory, and potentially as much as 48 gigabytes' worth of it. The system will likely not have that much in it, though, given the costs of DRAM these days.

Is Microsoft a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 32 Buys and four Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 2.03% loss in its share price over the past year, the average MSFT price target of $593.38 per share implies 49.47% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Fall as AI Disruption Fears Continue to Drag Down Tech Stocks
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell on Tuesday morning, kicking off a holiday-shortened trading week in the red amid continued fears of artificial intelligence (AI) disrupting software and other businesses. Meanwhile, traders await key employment figures, FOMC minutes, and delayed economic reports. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.79%, 0.38%, and 0.15%, respectively, at 8:36 a.m. EST on February 17.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Last week, all three major indexes closed lower. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones each dropped more than 1%, while the Nasdaq Composite fell over 2%, marking its fifth straight week of losses. Ongoing concerns about AI disrupting software, real estate, and financial services weighed on investor sentiment.

Turning to trending tickers, shares of health tech giant Masimo (MASI) rallied more than 34% in pre-market trading following reports that the company was nearing a deal with Danaher (DHR) to be acquired for about $10 billion in cash. Danaher stock declined about 6% on the news. Meanwhile, Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) stock jumped 6% following a Wall Street Journal report that Elliott Investment Management has built a more than 10% stake in the company.

Also, Paramount Skydance (PSKY) stock rose about 3% after Netflix (NFLX) granted Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) a seven-day waiver to hold deal talks with Paramount. WBD stock gained 2% on the news.

data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: XRP Price Potential Was Stifled by Regulatory 'Winner-Picking,' According to Legal Experts
data.description:
XRP (XRP-USD) supporters and legal experts are once again diving into the history of the industry to ask if regulators picked winners through uneven enforcement. The debate between pro-XRP attorney Bill Morgan and former SEC lawyer Marc Fagel has reignited as the market looks back at the long-term impact of the now-settled Ripple lawsuit. While the legal battle officially ended in August 2025 with a $125 million settlement, the argument continues over whether the four-year delay allowed competing cryptocurrencies to gain an unshakable lead in adoption and market value.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Morgan Argues Selective Enforcement

The discussion began when Bill Morgan suggested that the SEC's early decisions may have shaped today's market structure. He argued that several early crypto projects held token launches or used aggressive market promotion but were never targeted by regulators. According to Morgan, this allowed a small number of assets to grow without the massive legal pressure that eventually hit Ripple. He believes this uneven approach gave specific cryptocurrencies a long-term advantage, essentially helping them capture a lion's share of the market while XRP was stuck in a courtroom.

Fagel Defends Regulatory Logic

Marc Fagel pushed back on the idea that the SEC was intentionally picking winners. He explained that the commission cannot bring a securities case unless it can identify a clear company or "issuer" responsible for the asset.

In the case of Bitcoin, there was no central entity to sue, which made enforcement logically impossible. Fagel also argued that even if the SEC had the resources to go after every single project, those missing cases would not change the facts of whether Ripple itself broke securities laws. He noted that Ripple's own legal team argued in court that most XRP buyers were not actually relying on the company's efforts, which makes it harder to blame the SEC for the token's price performance.

Did the Legal Cloud Actually Hurt XRP's Growth?

The two attorneys also disagreed on how much the legal cloud actually hurt XRP's growth. Morgan emphasized that because Ripple was building almost all the early real-world use cases for the asset, a lawsuit against the company was effectively a lawsuit against the token's utility. He pointed out that while XRP often follows Bitcoin's price moves, the legal battle undeniably damaged investor perception and caused many U.S. exchanges to delist the token for years.

Even with the current regulatory clarity and the launch of new XRP ETFs, supporters like Morgan believe the lawsuit excuse has only recently run its course, leaving the asset to finally compete on a level playing field.

Key Takeaway

Essentially, this is a "chicken or the egg" argument that will likely never end. Supporters of XRP feel like the government put them in a cage for four years while everyone else was allowed to run free. On the other side, regulators and their defenders say they were just following the law where they could actually find someone to sue. Now that the handcuffs are off and the fine has been paid, the real test is beginning. If XRP can't regain its old spot near the top of the charts now that it has legal clarity, it might prove that the market has simply moved on to newer things.

At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $1.47.
data.author: Annika Masrani
